<html class="scroll-smooth" lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8"/>
<meta content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" name="viewport"/>
<title>Internal Memorandum: Brazil LNG &amp; LPG Market</title>
<script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;500;600;700&amp;display=swap" rel="stylesheet"/>
<style>
        body { font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; background-color: #f3f4f6; color: #1f2937; }
        .memo-container { max-width: 1280px; margin: 0 auto; }
        .memo-header { border-bottom: 4px solid #1e3a8a; }
        .toc-link { transition: all 0.2s ease; }
        .toc-link:hover { color: #1e3a8a; padding-left: 0.25rem; }
        .chart-bar { transition: width 1s ease-in-out; }
        
        /* Custom scrollbar for a cleaner look */
        ::-webkit-scrollbar { width: 8px; }
        ::-webkit-scrollbar-track { background: #f1f1f1; }
        ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { background: #cbd5e1; border-radius: 4px; }
        ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover { background: #94a3b8; }
        
        /* Print Styles for PDF Generation */
        @media print {
            :root { color-scheme: light !important; }
            html, body { 
                background: #ffffff !important;
                color: #000000 !important;
                display: block !important;
                height: auto !important;
                min-height: auto !important;
                overflow: visible !important;
            }
            * {
                -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact !important;
                print-color-adjust: exact !important;
            }
            @page { margin: 1.5cm; }
            
            /* Force block layout to fix Firefox pagination bug */
            .memo-container, main, article, section {
                display: block !important;
                width: 100% !important;
                max-width: none !important;
                height: auto !important;
                overflow: visible !important;
                box-shadow: none !important;
                float: none !important;
                position: static !important;
            }
            aside { display: none !important; }
            h2, h3, h4 { 
                page-break-after: avoid; 
                break-after: avoid; 
            }
            .memo-container { page-break-inside: auto; }
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body class="antialiased py-8 px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8 print:py-0 print:px-0 print:bg-white text-slate-800">
<div class="memo-container bg-white shadow-xl rounded-xl flex flex-col md:flex-row print:block print:shadow-none print:bg-white print:m-0 print:rounded-none" style="overflow: visible !important;">
<!-- Left Sidebar: TOC & Quick Actions -->
<aside class="w-full md:w-1/4 bg-slate-50 border-r border-slate-200 p-6 hidden md:block relative print:hidden">
<div class="sticky top-6">
<div class="mb-8">
<img alt="MISC Group" class="h-10 opacity-80 mb-6 grayscale hover:grayscale-0 transition duration-300" onerror="this.style.display='none'" src="data:image/png;base64,"/ style="background:transparent;">
<h2 class="text-xs font-bold text-slate-400 uppercase tracking-wider mb-4">Table of Contents</h2>
<nav class="space-y-3 text-sm font-medium text-slate-600"><a class="block toc-link" href="#exec-summary">1. Executive Takeaways</a><a class="block toc-link" href="#regulatory">2. Regulatory Backdrop</a><a class="block toc-link" href="#lng-mechanics">3. LNG: Mechanics That Matter for Shipping</a><a class="block toc-link" href="#lpg-mechanics">4. LPG: Mechanics That Matter for Shipping</a><a class="block toc-link" href="#ops-lens">5. Operator / Shipping Lens: Practical Implications</a><div class="ml-3 space-y-2 text-xs text-slate-500"><a class="block toc-link" href="#drought-congestion">5.1 Predictive Heuristic: The Drought-Congestion Correlation</a><a class="block toc-link" href="#cabotage-go-no-go">5.2 Cabotage: The Go / No-Go Decision &amp; The Portfolio Effect</a><a class="block toc-link" href="#implications-aet">5.3 Implications for AET (explicit operator actions)</a><a class="block toc-link" href="#outlook">5.4 6–12 Month Outlook (planning stance)</a></div><a class="block toc-link" href="#risk-matrix">6. Risk Registry &amp; Decision Matrix</a><a class="block toc-link" href="#appendix">7. Appendix</a><div class="ml-3 space-y-2 text-xs text-slate-500"><a class="block toc-link" href="#acronyms">7.1 Acronyms</a><a class="block toc-link" href="#kpi-dashboard">7.2 KPI &amp; Early-Warning Dashboard (operators)</a><a class="block toc-link" href="#source-set">7.3 Source Set</a></div></nav>
</div>
</div>
</aside>
<!-- Main Content Area -->
<main class="w-full md:w-3/4 p-8 lg:p-12 print:w-full print:p-0 print:block">
<!-- Print-Only Action Box (Visible only on PDF/Print) -->
<div class="hidden print:block bg-blue-50 border border-blue-200 rounded-lg p-5 mb-8">
<h3 class="text-blue-900 font-bold text-sm uppercase tracking-wide mb-3 flex items-center">
<svg class="w-4 h-4 mr-2" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewbox="0 0 24 24"><path d="M12 9v2m0 4h.01m-6.938 4h13.856c1.54 0 2.502-1.667 1.732-3L13.732 4c-.77-1.333-2.694-1.333-3.464 0L3.34 16c-.77 1.333.192 3 1.732 3z" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-width="2"></path></svg>
                    Action Required
                </h3>
<ul class="text-xs text-blue-800 space-y-2 list-disc pl-4">
<li>Multi-terminal discharge planning across expanded Floating Storage and Regasification Unit (FSRU) portfolio.</li>
<li>Broaden counterparty risk screening beyond Petrobras.</li>
<li>Treat dry-season months as "compressed delivery" periods with elevated demurrage.</li>
<li>Evaluate coastal redistribution gated by BR do Mar (Cabotage Transport Stimulation Program) compliance.</li>
<li>Formalize a go/no-go cabotage decision before coastal commitments.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<!-- Memo Header -->
<div class="memo-header pb-6 mb-8">
<h1 class="text-3xl font-extrabold text-slate-900 mb-6 tracking-tight">INTERNAL MEMORANDUM</h1>
<div class="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-12 gap-y-2 text-sm">
<div class="col-span-2 font-bold text-slate-500 uppercase">TO:</div>
<div class="col-span-10 text-slate-800 font-medium">CAPT. Chetan Budhraja <span class="text-slate-500 font-normal">| Head, MISC Group Liaison Office (Japan/Korea/China), Commercial Operations Gas Assets &amp; Solutions</span></div>
<div class="col-span-2 font-bold text-slate-500 uppercase">FROM:</div>
<div class="col-span-10 text-slate-800 font-medium">Pedro Vinicius Ruiz Lopes <span class="text-slate-500 font-normal">| Senior Vessel Operator, AET Tankers Brazil, Commercial Operations</span></div>
<div class="col-span-2 font-bold text-slate-500 uppercase">DATE:</div>
<div class="col-span-10 text-slate-800">22 February 2026</div>
<div class="col-span-2 font-bold text-slate-500 uppercase">TOPIC:</div>
<div class="col-span-10 text-slate-900 font-bold">What is structurally changing in Brazil's LNG/LPG flows and what it means for shipping (2026-2030)</div>
</div>
</div>
<!-- Content Sections -->
<article class="prose prose-slate max-w-none text-slate-700 leading-relaxed">
<!-- Section 1 -->
<section class="mb-12 scroll-mt-12" id="exec-summary">
<h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-slate-900 mb-4 border-b pb-2">1. Executive Takeaways</h2>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-6 mb-2">Structural Themes</h3>
<ul class="space-y-3 list-disc pl-5">
<li><strong class="text-slate-900">LNG imports are structurally growing and structurally multi-node.</strong> Brazil's LNG regasification capacity expanded from ~2.5 Bcf/d (2020) to ~5.1 Bcf/d (Aug 2025) as the market moved toward multiple coastal entry points rather than a single dominant hub. Operationally, this increases repeatable, multi-terminal discharge demand and forces fleet/operations to plan for terminal substitution and nomination discipline.</li>
<li><strong class="text-slate-900">The US Gulf–Brazil lane remains the dominant routing anchor.</strong> The U.S. supplied 79% of Brazil's LNG imports in 2023 and about 72% in 2024, reinforcing a direct Atlantic trade lane with reduced canal chokepoint dependency versus Pacific sourcing.</li>
<li><strong class="text-slate-900">Market structure has fragmented beyond Petrobras.</strong> New private terminal-to-power sponsors (e.g., NFE-linked Barcarena; GNA-linked Açu) expand the counterparty set and shift the risk posture from "single incumbent" to "portfolio of project-financed entities," requiring differentiated credit/ops screening.</li>
<li><strong class="text-slate-900">LPG is household-critical and politically non-negotiable.</strong> The federal "People's Gas" program is designed to reach &gt;17 million low-income families (~50 million people) with free LPG cylinder refills, making supply disruptions politically intolerable.</li>
<li><strong class="text-slate-900">MISC Berhad's 20-year LNG carrier commitment (Feb 2026).</strong> Signals fleet-level conviction in the long-haul LNG trade. Letter of Award (LOA) from PETRONAS LNG Ltd for three newbuild LNG carriers anchors the US Gulf–Brazil lane as a sustained structural trade route, not a cyclical opportunity.</li>
</ul>
<div class="bg-slate-100 border-l-4 border-slate-500 p-4 mt-6 text-sm italic rounded-r-lg">
<strong>Manning &amp; Veto Impacts:</strong> A proposed 2/3 Brazilian crew requirement for certain long-term contexts was vetoed; workforce requirements therefore fall back to general migration/crewing rules. Separately, BR do Mar participation can impose Brazilian senior officer requirements from day one (Captain/Chief Engineer roles), which is the binding constraint for LNG/LPG specialized tonnage.
                    </div>
</section>
<!-- Section 2 -->
<section class="mb-12 scroll-mt-12" id="regulatory">
<h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-slate-900 mb-4 border-b pb-2">2. Regulatory Backdrop</h2>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-4">2.1 New Gas Law and Third-Party Access (TPA) Regulatory Friction</h3>
<p>The New Gas Law underpins a shift to an authorization-based model and reinforces third-party access principles, enabling more shippers and terminal operators to enter without Petrobras mediation. The practical shipping consequence is the acceleration of a multi-sponsor terminal environment, increasing operational variability in nomination discipline, terminal interface readiness, and dispute friction.</p>
<p><strong>Active TPA Friction:</strong> Agência Nacional do Petróleo, Gás Natural e Biocombustíveis (ANP)'s 2025–2026 regulatory agenda centers on finalizing criteria to legally distinguish 'Transport Pipelines' from 'Production Flow Pipelines'. An unresolved classification dispute creates friction for multi-terminal planning: if upstream flow pipelines feeding a regas terminal are reclassified, the legal certainty of access terms for new shippers is affected.</p>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-6">2.2 Power System Hydrology and the LNG "Call Option"</h3>
<p>Brazil's power system is renewable-heavy and drought-exposed; LNG-to-power configurations make LNG the flexible balancing tool when hydrology tightens. The shipping implication is a two-layer demand structure: Baseload (contracted) and Weather-driven incremental cargoes.</p>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-6">2.3 Resolução Normativa CNIG/MJSP No. 42 (RN42), BR do Mar, and the Dynamic Positioning Shuttle Tanker (DPST) Fleet Reality</h3>
<p>RN42 establishes two distinct quota scales depending on vessel type. AET's DPSTs operating in Brazilian waters fall under Art. 4° III — the lighter scale applicable to cabotage and LNG regasification vessels. Operative requirements: <strong>1/5 Brazilian crew from 90+ days, rising to 1/3 from 180+ days.</strong></p>
<p><strong>BR do Mar Interaction:</strong> BR do Mar (Law 14,301/2022) opened participation to foreign-owned tonnage. The key crewing implication is that vessels operating under BR do Mar arrangements requiring Brazilian flag registration become subject to the Law 9,432/97 Brazilian Captain and Chief Engineer requirement. This is the crewing threshold that would need to be cleared for any LNG/LPG coastal redeployment strategy.</p>
</section>
<!-- Section 3 -->
<section class="mb-12 scroll-mt-12" id="lng-mechanics">
<h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-slate-900 mb-4 border-b pb-2">3. LNG: Mechanics That Matter for Shipping</h2>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-4 mb-4">3.1 Regasification Capacity Growth: 2020 → 2026</h3>
<p>Four structural drivers combine to sustain LNG import demand: FSRU speed-to-market, Hydrology volatility, Integrated LNG-to-power locking demand, and Multiple coastal demand pockets.</p>
<!-- CSS Data Visualization: Capacity Bar Chart -->
<div class="bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-xl p-6 my-6 shadow-sm break-inside-avoid">
<h4 class="text-sm font-bold text-slate-500 uppercase mb-4 text-center">Brazil LNG Regasification Capacity (Bcf/d)</h4>
<div class="space-y-4">
<!-- 2020 -->
<div class="flex items-center">
<div class="w-20 text-sm font-semibold text-slate-700">2020</div>
<div class="flex-1 bg-slate-100 rounded-full h-6 overflow-hidden relative group">
<div class="chart-bar bg-blue-300 h-full rounded-full" style="width: 43%;"></div>
</div>
<div class="w-16 text-right text-sm font-bold text-slate-700">~2.5</div>
</div>
<!-- 2022 -->
<div class="flex items-center">
<div class="w-20 text-sm font-semibold text-slate-700">2022</div>
<div class="flex-1 bg-slate-100 rounded-full h-6 overflow-hidden relative">
<div class="chart-bar bg-blue-500 h-full rounded-full" style="width: 56%;"></div>
</div>
<div class="w-16 text-right text-sm font-bold text-slate-700">~3.3</div>
</div>
<!-- 2024 -->
<div class="flex items-center">
<div class="w-20 text-sm font-semibold text-slate-700">2024</div>
<div class="flex-1 bg-slate-100 rounded-full h-6 overflow-hidden relative">
<div class="chart-bar bg-blue-700 h-full rounded-full" style="width: 74%;"></div>
</div>
<div class="w-16 text-right text-sm font-bold text-slate-700">~4.3</div>
</div>
<!-- 2025 -->
<div class="flex items-center">
<div class="w-20 text-sm font-semibold text-slate-700">Aug 2025</div>
<div class="flex-1 bg-slate-100 rounded-full h-6 overflow-hidden relative">
<div class="chart-bar bg-blue-900 h-full rounded-full" style="width: 87%;"></div>
</div>
<div class="w-16 text-right text-sm font-bold text-slate-700">~5.1</div>
</div>
<!-- 2026 -->
<div class="flex items-center">
<div class="w-20 text-sm font-semibold text-amber-600">Est. 2026</div>
<div class="flex-1 bg-slate-100 rounded-full h-6 overflow-hidden relative">
<div class="chart-bar bg-amber-500 h-full rounded-full" style="width: 100%;"></div>
</div>
<div class="w-16 text-right text-sm font-bold text-amber-600">~5.8</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-xl p-6 my-6 shadow-sm break-inside-avoid">
<h4 class="text-sm font-bold text-slate-500 uppercase mb-4 text-center">Chart Pack (for copy/paste into slides)</h4>
<div class="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-6">
<div>
<h5 class="text-xs font-bold text-slate-500 uppercase mb-2">Regasification capacity trend</h5>
<img alt="Regasification capacity trend" class="w-full rounded-lg border border-slate-200" src="data:image/png;base64,"/>
<p class="text-[10px] text-slate-500 mt-2">Source: memo values (2020–Est. 2026).</p>
</div>
<div>
<h5 class="text-xs font-bold text-slate-500 uppercase mb-2">U.S. share of Brazil LNG imports</h5>
<img alt="US share of Brazil LNG imports" class="w-full rounded-lg border border-slate-200" src="data:image/png;base64,"/>
<p class="text-[10px] text-slate-500 mt-2">Source: memo statement (79% in 2023; ~72% in 2024).</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-8 mb-4">3.2 The North-to-South Routing Arc</h3>
<p>The operational reality of Brazil's LNG network has shifted from a concentrated hub to a vast coastal corridor. This requires multi-terminal discharge planning.</p>
<div class="flex flex-col md:flex-row gap-8 items-start my-6 bg-slate-50 p-6 rounded-xl border border-slate-200 break-inside-avoid">
<!-- Node Timeline / SVG Map -->
<div class="w-full md:w-1/3 flex justify-center border-r border-slate-200 pr-4">
<svg height="400" viewbox="0 0 200 400" width="220" xmlns="">http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"&gt;
                                <defs>
<lineargradient id="lineGrad" x1="0%" x2="0%" y1="0%" y2="100%">
<stop offset="0%" stop-color="#1e3a8a"></stop>
<stop offset="100%" stop-color="#3b82f6"></stop>
</lineargradient>
</defs>
<!-- Main Line -->
<line stroke="url(#lineGrad)" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-width="4" x1="40" x2="40" y1="30" y2="370"></line>
<!-- Nodes & Labels -->
<!-- Barcarena -->
<circle cx="40" cy="40" fill="#fff" r="8" stroke="#1e3a8a" stroke-width="3"></circle>
<text fill="#1e293b" font-family="Inter, sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" x="60" y="45">PA: Barcarena</text>
<!-- Suape -->
<circle cx="40" cy="95" fill="#fff" r="8" stroke="#1e3a8a" stroke-width="3"></circle>
<text fill="#1e293b" font-family="Inter, sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" x="60" y="100">PE: Suape (Est. '26)</text>
<!-- Bahia -->
<circle cx="40" cy="150" fill="#fff" r="8" stroke="#1e3a8a" stroke-width="3"></circle>
<text fill="#1e293b" font-family="Inter, sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" x="60" y="155">BA: Bahia/Salvador</text>
<!-- Sergipe -->
<circle cx="40" cy="205" fill="#fff" r="8" stroke="#1e3a8a" stroke-width="3"></circle>
<text fill="#1e293b" font-family="Inter, sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" x="60" y="210">SE: Sergipe</text>
<!-- RJ Complex -->
<circle cx="40" cy="260" fill="#f59e0b" r="10" stroke="#fff" stroke-width="2"></circle>
<text fill="#1e293b" font-family="Inter, sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" x="60" y="255">RJ: Guanabara / </text>
<text fill="#475569" font-family="Inter, sans-serif" font-size="11" x="60" y="270">Sepetiba / Açu (Core Hub)</text>
<!-- Cosan -->
<circle cx="40" cy="315" fill="#fff" r="8" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="3"></circle>
<text fill="#1e293b" font-family="Inter, sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" x="60" y="320">SP: Cosan</text>
<!-- Gas Sul -->
<circle cx="40" cy="370" fill="#fff" r="8" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="3"></circle>
<text fill="#1e293b" font-family="Inter, sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" x="60" y="375">SC: Terminal Gás Sul</text>
</svg>
</div>
<!-- Terminal Table -->
<div class="w-full md:w-2/3 overflow-x-auto text-sm">
<table class="min-w-full divide-y divide-slate-200">
<thead>
<tr>
<th class="px-3 py-2 text-left text-xs font-medium text-slate-500 uppercase">Terminal</th>
<th class="px-3 py-2 text-left text-xs font-medium text-slate-500 uppercase">Status</th>
<th class="px-3 py-2 text-left text-xs font-medium text-slate-500 uppercase">Shipping Lens</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody class="divide-y divide-slate-200">
<tr><td class="px-3 py-2 font-medium">Barcarena (PA)</td><td class="px-3 py-2 text-green-600 font-bold">OPER.</td><td class="px-3 py-2 text-slate-600">New hub; pair ops readiness with power ramp.</td></tr>
<tr><td class="px-3 py-2 font-medium">Sergipe (SE)</td><td class="px-3 py-2 text-green-600 font-bold">OPER.</td><td class="px-3 py-2 text-slate-600">Dispatch-driven: deviations map to generation gaps.</td></tr>
<tr><td class="px-3 py-2 font-medium">Açu/Guanabara (RJ)</td><td class="px-3 py-2 text-green-600 font-bold">OPER.</td><td class="px-3 py-2 text-slate-600">Congestion zones; plan substitution options.</td></tr>
<tr><td class="px-3 py-2 font-medium">Suape (PE)</td><td class="px-3 py-2 text-amber-500 font-bold">EST '26</td><td class="px-3 py-2 text-slate-600">Do not lock tight windows until 3-6 mos stable.</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>
</div>
</section>
<!-- Section 4 -->
<section class="mb-12 scroll-mt-12" id="lpg-mechanics">
<h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-slate-900 mb-4 border-b pb-2">4. LPG: Mechanics That Matter for Shipping</h2>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-4">4.1 Demand Structure — “Politically Non‑Negotiable” Means Operationally Non‑Optional</h3>
<p>Brazil’s LPG demand is now policy‑locked, not purely commercial. The federal <strong>People’s Gas (Gás do Povo)</strong> program provides free LPG cylinder refills to &gt;17 million low‑income families (~50 million people), targeting <strong>65 million cylinders</strong> distributed by end‑2026 (~R$6.75bn value). This is structurally different from discretionary retail demand: supply disruptions become immediately visible and politically intolerable.</p>
<p><strong>Volume Translation (shipping relevance):</strong> 65 million standard 13kg cylinders translate to roughly <strong>845,000 metric tons</strong> of LPG. Given standard <strong>Very Large Gas Carrier (VLGC)</strong> parcel size (~45,000 metric tons), this social program alone equates to approximately <strong>18–19 additional VLGC cargoes per year</strong> into Brazil’s primary import / redistribution corridors. This makes LPG reliability a quasi‑sovereign constraint (government historically absorbs fiscal/logistical costs to avoid visible shortages), not merely a commercial service-level issue.</p>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-6">4.2 Lane Structure — The Emerging Vaca Muerta Shift Changes Routing Assumptions</h3>
<p>Argus (late‑2025) reported Brazil’s LPG imports from the U.S. are losing market share to Argentina as <strong>Vaca Muerta‑sourced LPG</strong> offers shorter haul distance and competitive delivered pricing. This diversification changes the default “anchor lane” assumption and introduces higher nomination variability.</p>
<ul class="space-y-3 list-disc pl-5">
<li><strong>Lane diversity → nomination variability:</strong> shorter-haul Atlantic parcels tighten the time-to-arrival window, increasing sensitivity to berth readiness and sequencing.</li>
<li><strong>Contingency routing must be pre‑approved:</strong> because disruptions are politically high‑salience, alternates (terminal + distributor + discharge window) should be maintained as an always‑ready playbook, not an exception workflow.</li>
<li><strong>Cargo mix implications:</strong> different propane/butane ratios and parcel fragmentation can increase time in port if segregation or blending is constrained by storage.</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-6">4.3 Terminal &amp; Vessel Interface — LPG’s Constraint Stack (vs. LNG)</h3>
<p>Unlike LNG where regasification capacity and FSRU scheduling dominate, LPG constraints concentrate around berth availability, storage ullage, and downstream evacuation (bulk trucking/bottling/coastal redistribution). Operationally:</p>
<ul class="space-y-3 list-disc pl-5">
<li><strong>Storage dominates turnaround:</strong> discharge is often gated by tank line‑up and evacuation rate; port time and demurrage risk rise when downstream bottlenecks appear.</li>
<li><strong>Segregation matters:</strong> off‑spec mix or constrained segregation drives re‑blending delays and berth occupancy.</li>
<li><strong>Operating envelope (berth/daylight/STS):</strong> where lightering or ship‑to‑ship (STS) is used, define weather/daylight/tug constraints as dispatch critical path (analogous to LNG “terminal substitution” planning).</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-6">4.4 Risk Profile — Why LPG Demurrage Spikes When Politics Spikes</h3>
<p>Because LPG shortages translate directly to household cylinder availability, political stress compresses decision cycles and increases intervention probability. When supply is tight, expect forced re‑sequencing, priority allocations, and higher berth-time volatility. Treat dry-season months and policy-trigger periods as <em>compressed delivery windows</em> and bake buffer days into nominations.</p>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-6">4.5 Operator Playbook — Pre‑Decisions That Prevent Chaos</h3>
<ul class="space-y-3 list-disc pl-5">
<li><strong>Pre‑cleared alternates:</strong> at least one alternate discharge option per primary hub (terminal + distributor interface + window).</li>
<li><strong>Political priority trigger set:</strong> define indicators that auto‑escalate to management action (retail shortage signals, terminal ullage stress, lane disruptions).</li>
<li><strong>Counterparty readiness:</strong> map which buyers/terminals can accept rerouted parcels without renegotiation of discharge terms.</li>
<li><strong>Berth-time protection:</strong> treat delays as systemic; build buffer days and demurrage sensitivity into planning.</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-6">4.6 Early‑Warning Dashboard (LPG‑specific)</h3>
<ul class="space-y-3 list-disc pl-5">
<li>Retail cylinder stress signals (availability / rationing / price spikes)</li>
<li>Terminal congestion markers (berth line‑up, storage utilization, evacuation throughput)</li>
<li>Lane disruption alerts (U.S. Gulf / Argentina export constraints; routing friction)</li>
</ul>
</section>

<h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-slate-900 mb-4 border-b pb-2" id="ops-lens">5. Operator / Shipping Lens: Practical Implications</h2>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-4" id="drought-congestion">5.1 Predictive Heuristic: The Drought-Congestion Correlation</h3>
<p>Brazil's port congestion is severe. During peak periods, demurrage rates surge. To operationalize the hydrology risk mentioned in Section 2.2, use this predictive heuristic:</p>
<div class="bg-yellow-50 border border-yellow-200 p-5 rounded-lg my-4 text-yellow-900">
<strong>Operational Rule of Thumb:</strong> When reservoir levels in the Southeast/Midwest subsystem drop below <strong>30%</strong>, immediate thermal dispatch penalties activate. Expect a rapid influx of incremental cargoes resulting in a <strong>5 to 10-day increase in LNG berth congestion</strong> at primary hubs like Guanabara Bay and Açu. 
                    </div>
<p>Build buffer days into dry-season nominations to absorb this queue risk, as "late window" demurrage exposure is highly non-linear.</p>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-8" id="cabotage-go-no-go">5.2 Cabotage: The Go / No-Go Decision &amp; The Portfolio Effect</h3>
<p>Entering the coastal redistribution market requires navigating BR do Mar and severe crewing bottlenecks for specialized LNG/LPG senior officers. However, MISC/AET possesses a unique structural advantage.</p>
<div class="bg-slate-800 text-white p-5 rounded-lg my-4 shadow-md">
<h4 class="text-blue-300 font-bold uppercase text-xs mb-2">Strategic Synthesis</h4>
<p class="mb-0 text-sm"><strong>The Portfolio Effect:</strong> AET currently manages a significant pool of Brazilian mariners for its DPST (shuttle tanker) fleet under RN42 quotas. By implementing a cross-training and dual-certification program now, we can leverage this existing workforce to bridge the senior officer bottleneck. This transforms a regulatory constraint (Law 9,432/97) into a competitive moat against foreign operators lacking an established Brazilian crewing pipeline.</p>
</div>
<p>Until that pipeline is secure, the decision framework stands as follows:</p>
<div class="overflow-hidden shadow ring-1 ring-black ring-opacity-5 rounded-lg my-6 break-inside-avoid">
<table class="min-w-full divide-y divide-slate-300">
<thead class="bg-slate-50">
<tr>
<th class="py-3.5 pl-4 pr-3 text-left text-sm font-semibold text-slate-900">Option</th>
<th class="px-3 py-3.5 text-left text-sm font-semibold text-slate-900">Description</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody class="divide-y divide-slate-200 bg-white">
<tr>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-4 pl-4 pr-3 text-sm font-medium text-slate-900">A. Acquire</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-slate-500">Purchase BR-flagged tonnage (highest capex; full market security)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-4 pl-4 pr-3 text-sm font-medium text-slate-900">B. Charter</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-slate-500">Use BR do Mar pathways (commercially flexible, but crewing constraints bite)</td>
</tr>
<tr class="bg-blue-50">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-4 pl-4 pr-3 text-sm font-bold text-blue-900">C. Decline (DEFAULT)</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-blue-800 font-medium">Focus on deep-sea import legs only. <strong>TRIGGER POINT:</strong> Maintain Option C <em>unless</em> the government introduces specific tax incentives for BR do Mar that mathematically offset the 20-30% higher operating cost of employing Brazilian senior officers.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-10" id="implications-aet">5.3 Implications for AET (explicit operator actions)</h3>
<div class="bg-slate-50 border border-slate-200 rounded-xl p-6 my-4 break-inside-avoid">
<ul class="space-y-3 list-disc pl-5 text-sm">
<li><strong class="text-slate-900">Institutionalize multi-terminal substitution planning.</strong> Maintain primary/secondary discharge options across the expanded coastal corridor and keep terminal interface readiness current.</li>
<li><strong class="text-slate-900">Upgrade counterparty screening beyond Petrobras defaults.</strong> Treat terminal-to-power sponsors and SPVs as heterogeneous entities with differing nomination discipline and operational readiness.</li>
<li><strong class="text-slate-900">Adopt a seasonal buffer policy for dry-season queues.</strong> Treat dry-season months as “compressed delivery” periods with nonlinear demurrage exposure; pre-load buffer days into nominations.</li>
<li><strong class="text-slate-900">Formalize the cabotage Go/No-Go gate before coastal commitments.</strong> Preserve the Acquire / Charter / Decline framework and document the “Trigger Point” conditions that justify switching out of the default posture.</li>
<li><strong class="text-slate-900">Turn RN42/BR do Mar constraints into an advantage via the Portfolio Effect.</strong> Execute cross-training and dual-certification pathways using the existing DPST Brazilian mariner pool to address senior officer bottlenecks for LNG/LPG tonnage.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-10" id="outlook">5.4 6–12 Month Outlook (planning stance)</h3>
<div class="bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-xl p-6 my-4 shadow-sm break-inside-avoid">
<div class="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-6 text-sm">
<div>
<h4 class="text-sm font-bold text-slate-700 uppercase mb-2">Hydrology-driven volatility</h4>
<p class="text-slate-700">Maintain nomination optionality and protect late-window exposure as LNG demand swings with hydrology (baseload + incremental layer).</p>
</div>
<div>
<h4 class="text-sm font-bold text-slate-700 uppercase mb-2">Terminal readiness / commissioning risk</h4>
<p class="text-slate-700">Apply a stability proof rule for new/transitioning terminals (e.g., Suape): avoid tight commitments until reliability is proven.</p>
</div>
<div>
<h4 class="text-sm font-bold text-slate-700 uppercase mb-2">LPG political priority</h4>
<p class="text-slate-700">Keep contingency routing and distributor/terminal alternates ready; treat priority re-route instructions as high-salience events.</p>
</div>
<div>
<h4 class="text-sm font-bold text-slate-700 uppercase mb-2">Cabotage remains gated</h4>
<p class="text-slate-700">Keep a “paper-ready” cabotage package (compliance + crewing + economics) so the business can move quickly if the Trigger Point shifts.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<!-- Section 6 -->
<section class="mb-12 scroll-mt-12" id="risk-matrix">
<h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-slate-900 mb-4 border-b pb-2">6. Risk Registry &amp; Decision Matrix</h2>
<p class="text-sm text-slate-500 mb-4">Scale: Probability 1–5 (rare → likely), Impact 1–5 (low → severe), Score = P × I.<br/>
<span class="inline-block w-3 h-3 bg-red-100 border border-red-300 mr-1"></span> Red: 15-25 (Critical) | <span class="inline-block w-3 h-3 bg-yellow-100 border border-yellow-300 mr-1"></span> Yellow: 8-14 (Moderate) | <span class="inline-block w-3 h-3 bg-green-100 border border-green-300 mr-1"></span> Green: &lt;8 (Low)</p>
<div class="bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-xl p-6 my-6 shadow-sm break-inside-avoid">
<h4 class="text-sm font-bold text-slate-500 uppercase mb-4 text-center">Risk Registry – Score Snapshot</h4>
<img alt="Risk registry score snapshot" class="w-full rounded-lg border border-slate-200" src="data:image/png;base64,"/>
<p class="text-[10px] text-slate-500 mt-2">Derived from the risk table below (Score = P×I).</p>
</div>
<div class="overflow-x-auto shadow ring-1 ring-black ring-opacity-5 rounded-lg">
<table class="min-w-full divide-y divide-slate-300">
<thead class="bg-slate-800">
<tr>
<th class="py-3.5 pl-4 pr-3 text-left text-xs font-semibold text-white uppercase tracking-wide">Risk</th>
<th class="px-3 py-3.5 text-center text-xs font-semibold text-white uppercase tracking-wide">P</th>
<th class="px-3 py-3.5 text-center text-xs font-semibold text-white uppercase tracking-wide">I</th>
<th class="px-3 py-3.5 text-center text-xs font-semibold text-white uppercase tracking-wide">Score</th>
<th class="px-3 py-3.5 text-left text-xs font-semibold text-white uppercase tracking-wide">Operational Mitigation / Next Action</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody class="divide-y divide-slate-200">
<tr class="bg-red-50 hover:bg-red-100 transition-colors">
<td class="py-4 pl-4 pr-3 text-sm font-medium text-slate-900">Dry-season demurrage / congestion</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold">4</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold">5</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold text-red-700">20</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-slate-700">Seasonal buffers; multi-terminal pre-qual.<br/><strong class="text-slate-900">Act:</strong> Identify which terminals operate year-round baseload vs. drought-response.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="bg-red-50 hover:bg-red-100 transition-colors">
<td class="py-4 pl-4 pr-3 text-sm font-medium text-slate-900">Counterparty credit dispersion</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold">4</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold">4</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold text-red-700">16</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-slate-700">Do not apply Petrobras assumptions to SPVs.<br/><strong class="text-slate-900">Act:</strong> Map projects with integrated PPA dispatch visibility.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="bg-yellow-50 hover:bg-yellow-100 transition-colors">
<td class="py-4 pl-4 pr-3 text-sm font-medium text-slate-900">Terminal commissioning instability (Suape)</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold">3</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold">4</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold text-yellow-700">12</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-slate-700">Avoid tight commitments until proven.<br/><strong class="text-slate-900">Act:</strong> Confirm operating envelope (Ship-to-Ship (STS)/berth/air draft).</td>
</tr>
<tr class="bg-yellow-50 hover:bg-yellow-100 transition-colors">
<td class="py-4 pl-4 pr-3 text-sm font-medium text-slate-900">BR senior officer sourcing bottleneck</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold">3</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold">4</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold text-yellow-700">12</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-slate-700">Leverage DPST portfolio cross-training.<br/><strong class="text-slate-900">Act:</strong> Audit current Brazilian pool for LNG/LPG qualification potential.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="bg-yellow-50 hover:bg-yellow-100 transition-colors">
<td class="py-4 pl-4 pr-3 text-sm font-medium text-slate-900">TPA / regulatory friction</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold">3</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold">3</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold text-yellow-700">9</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-slate-700">Plan alternates &amp; contractual protections.<br/><strong class="text-slate-900">Act:</strong> Track ANP pipeline dispute environment.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="bg-yellow-50 hover:bg-yellow-100 transition-colors">
<td class="py-4 pl-4 pr-3 text-sm font-medium text-slate-900">Gov't LPG intervention (priority/reroute)</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold">3</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold">3</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-center font-bold text-yellow-700">9</td>
<td class="px-3 py-4 text-sm text-slate-700">Pre-approved contingency playbooks.<br/><strong class="text-slate-900">Act:</strong> Pre-qualify alternate terminals and distributor contacts.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>
</section>
<!-- Section 7 -->
<section class="mb-12 scroll-mt-12" id="appendix">
<h2 class="text-2xl font-bold text-slate-900 mb-4 border-b pb-2">7. Appendix</h2><div data-moved-block="acronyms"><h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-4 mb-3" id="acronyms">7.1 Acronyms</h3>
<div class="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-4 text-xs mb-8">
<div class="bg-white p-3 border border-slate-200 rounded-lg shadow-sm"><strong>ANP:</strong> Agência Nacional do Petróleo, Gás Natural e Biocombustíveis</div>
<div class="bg-white p-3 border border-slate-200 rounded-lg shadow-sm"><strong>Bcf/d:</strong> Billion cubic feet per day</div>
<div class="bg-white p-3 border border-slate-200 rounded-lg shadow-sm"><strong>BR do Mar:</strong> Cabotage Transport Stimulation Program (Law 14,301/2022)</div>
<div class="bg-white p-3 border border-slate-200 rounded-lg shadow-sm"><strong>COA:</strong> Contract of Affreightment</div>
<div class="bg-white p-3 border border-slate-200 rounded-lg shadow-sm"><strong>FSRU:</strong> Floating Storage and Regasification Unit</div>
<div class="bg-white p-3 border border-slate-200 rounded-lg shadow-sm"><strong>LGC/VLGC:</strong> Large / Very Large Gas Carrier</div>
<div class="bg-white p-3 border border-slate-200 rounded-lg shadow-sm"><strong>LNG/LPG:</strong> Liquefied Natural Gas / Liquefied Petroleum Gas</div>
<div class="bg-white p-3 border border-slate-200 rounded-lg shadow-sm"><strong>MME:</strong> Ministério de Minas e Energia</div>
<div class="bg-white p-3 border border-slate-200 rounded-lg shadow-sm"><strong>PPA:</strong> Power Purchase Agreement</div>
<div class="bg-white p-3 border border-slate-200 rounded-lg shadow-sm"><strong>RN42:</strong> Resolução Normativa CNIG/MJSP No. 42 (Immigration Council resolution)</div>
<div class="bg-white p-3 border border-slate-200 rounded-lg shadow-sm"><strong>SPV:</strong> Special Purpose Vehicle</div>
<div class="bg-white p-3 border border-slate-200 rounded-lg shadow-sm"><strong>STS:</strong> Ship-to-Ship (transfer operation)</div>
<div class="bg-white p-3 border border-slate-200 rounded-lg shadow-sm md:col-span-2 lg:col-span-3"><strong>UA / TPA:</strong> Use Agreement / Third-Party Access (terminal governance)</div>
</div>
</div><div data-moved-block="kpi-dashboard"><h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-10 mb-3" id="kpi-dashboard">7.2 KPI &amp; Early-Warning Dashboard (operators)</h3>
<div class="bg-slate-50 border border-slate-200 rounded-xl p-6 shadow-sm break-inside-avoid">
<p class="text-sm text-slate-600 mb-4">Convert key rules of thumb and risk drivers into monitorable indicators:</p>
<div class="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-6 text-sm">
<div>
<h4 class="text-xs font-bold text-slate-600 uppercase mb-2">Hydrology → LNG queue risk</h4>
<ul class="list-disc pl-5 space-y-2">
<li><strong>SE/Midwest reservoir level (%)</strong>: Trigger when below <strong>30%</strong> (expect 5–10 day congestion increase at primary hubs).</li>
<li><strong>Dry-season flag</strong>: Apply seasonal buffer days and elevate demurrage sensitivity (“compressed delivery” months).</li>
</ul>
</div>
<div>
<h4 class="text-xs font-bold text-slate-600 uppercase mb-2">Terminal readiness</h4>
<ul class="list-disc pl-5 space-y-2">
<li><strong>Suape stability window</strong>: Avoid tight windows until stable operations are demonstrated (memo suggests 3–6 months stability proof).</li>
<li><strong>Terminal substitution readiness</strong>: Keep secondary discharge approvals current across the corridor.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<div>
<h4 class="text-xs font-bold text-slate-600 uppercase mb-2">Counterparty / credit dispersion</h4>
<ul class="list-disc pl-5 space-y-2">
<li><strong>Counterparty class</strong>: Petrobras vs SPV/terminal-to-power sponsor; do not apply incumbent assumptions to SPVs.</li>
<li><strong>Dispatch visibility proxy</strong>: Track which projects have integrated PPA/dispatch visibility vs merchant exposure.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<div>
<h4 class="text-xs font-bold text-slate-600 uppercase mb-2">Regulatory &amp; cabotage gate</h4>
<ul class="list-disc pl-5 space-y-2">
<li><strong>ANP TPA friction tracker</strong>: Monitor pipeline classification disputes impacting access certainty.</li>
<li><strong>Cabotage Trigger Point</strong>: Maintain “Decline (DEFAULT)” unless incentives offset higher Brazilian senior-officer costs.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<div>
<h4 class="text-xs font-bold text-slate-600 uppercase mb-2">Crewing capacity (binding constraint)</h4>
<ul class="list-disc pl-5 space-y-2">
<li><strong>RN42 quotas</strong>: 1/5 BR crew from 90+ days; 1/3 from 180+ days for applicable vessels.</li>
<li><strong>Senior officer availability</strong>: Track Captain/Chief Engineer constraints when BR do Mar pathways apply.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<div>
<h4 class="text-xs font-bold text-slate-600 uppercase mb-2">LPG political priority</h4>
<ul class="list-disc pl-5 space-y-2">
<li><strong>People’s Gas rollout</strong>: Treat reroutes/priority calls as high-salience events; keep contingency playbooks ready.</li>
<li><strong>Sourcing shift</strong>: Monitor drift toward Argentina/Vaca Muerta lanes vs US Gulf dominance.</li>
</ul>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div><div data-moved-block="source-set"><h3 class="text-lg font-semibold text-slate-800 mt-4 mb-3" id="source-set">7.3 Source Set</h3>
<div class="overflow-hidden shadow ring-1 ring-black ring-opacity-5 rounded-lg">
<table class="min-w-full divide-y divide-slate-300">
<thead class="bg-slate-50">
<tr>
<th class="py-3 pl-4 pr-3 text-left text-xs font-semibold text-slate-900 uppercase">Source / Domain</th>
<th class="px-3 py-3 text-left text-xs font-semibold text-slate-900 uppercase">Date</th>
<th class="px-3 py-3 text-left text-xs font-semibold text-slate-900 uppercase">Content Used</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody class="divide-y divide-slate-200 bg-white text-xs">
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">U.S. EIA / eia.gov</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">Oct 2025</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">Brazil regas capacity growth; Suape '26; LNG-to-power linkages</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">Global Energy Monitor</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">2025</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">Suape FSRU project details; operating year 2026 indication</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">New Fortress Energy IR</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">Oct 2025</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">CELBA 2 / Barcarena first fire milestone; PortoCem timeline</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">MME (gov.br)</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">2025</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">Gás do Povo: 17M families / ~50M people target; March 2026 rollout</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">U.S. Commercial Service</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">Aug 2025</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">U.S. LNG supply share: 79% (2023), ~72% (2024)</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">BR do Mar Program</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">Jan 2026</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">Program intent, chartering structure, cabotage stimulation framework</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">Natural Gas Intelligence</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">2025</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">LNG-to-power drought-response dynamics; incremental cargo context</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">MISC Berhad / Bursa Malaysia</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">3 Feb 2026</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">LOA for 3 newbuild LNGCs on 20-year charters (delivery 2029)</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">CNIG/MJSP No. 42 (RN42)</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">23 Jul 2020</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">Progressive Brazilian crew quota requirements (Art. 4º)</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">Argus Media</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">2 Jan 2026</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">Viewpoint: Global LNG supply wave; Petrobras Sergipe-Alagoas delay</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">ANP / gov.br/anp</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">29 Dec 2025</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">Revised pipeline tariff regime: entry-exit model, WACC update</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">Argus Media</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">3 Dec 2025</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">Gás do Povo distributor pricing to R$103.93/cyl; regional tiers</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">IISD</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">2012</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">Historical LPG subsidies from 1960s; single pricing policy</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">Petrobras</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">10 Feb 2026</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">Q4 2025 production 3.11M boed; Búzios crossed 1M bpd</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">DatamarNews / Folha</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">2025</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">84% Santos vessels delayed; ~$30k/day demurrage surcharges</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">Argus Media</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">14 Nov 2025</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">Brazil shifting LPG imports toward Argentina (Vaca Muerta)</td>
</tr>
<tr class="hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors">
<td class="whitespace-nowrap py-3 pl-4 pr-3 font-medium text-slate-900">ANP / IBP Open Letter</td>
<td class="whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-3 text-slate-500">2025</td>
<td class="px-3 py-3 text-slate-600">Classification of Transport vs Production Flow Pipelines</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>
</div>
</section>
</article>
<!-- Footer -->
<footer class="mt-16 pt-8 border-t border-slate-200 text-center text-sm text-slate-400">
<p>Commercial Operations Department | AET Tankers Brazil</p>
<p>Confidential Internal Document - Generated Feb 2026</p>
</footer>
</main>
</div>
</body>
</html>
